understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to move pushable blocks onto goal tiles (marked with '+') or otherwise satisfy a specific success condition for each level, which is signaled by a WIN.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was grid-based, using pixel coordinates; you could move the currently selected piece in four directions unless blocked by walls or objects. Pushable blocks could be moved by pushing them with the selected piece. Walls and other boundaries could block or limit movement differently, so experimentation was required. Multiple selectable pieces could be switched between using CLICK with a given position.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to move pushable blocks onto goal tiles (marked with '+') or otherwise satisfy a specific success condition for each level, which is signaled by a WIN.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was grid-based, using pixel coordinates; you could move the currently selected piece in four directions unless blocked by walls or objects. Pushable blocks could be moved by pushing them with the selected piece. Walls and other boundaries could block or limit movement differently, so experimentation was required. Multiple selectable pieces could be switched between using CLICK with a given position.
